element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Active Time Battle 2.0 system teaches player timing dependency gradually
Foundation for all combat before special techniques
Special area-of-effect abilities with MP costs teach tactical positioning
Optional stat progression available throughout game
Resource management teaching through treasure chest discovery
Removes random encounters; player controls engagement timing and positioning
Scaled overhead view teaches spatial awareness and exploration
Environmental challenges teach observation and interaction beyond combat
Unique abilities per character teach team composition strategy
Core narrative mechanic introduces multiple eras with distinct challenges
Character development side-quests allow player-paced progression

typical_relief_triggers: Safe haven towns with shops and NPCs
typical_tension_triggers: Visible enemies on field maps requiring evasion or engagement decision
Time pressure from narrative catastrophe (Lavos threat)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Progressive escalation through time periods; punctuated equilibrium with safe towns offering respite between dungeon/battle sequences; multiple endings provide variable late-game pacing

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Off-path treasure chests discoverable in field maps; side quests tied to specific NPCs for character development

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction through basic combat and visible enemy placement system; teaches Active Time Battle fundamentals
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Escalation as time travel introduces multiple eras with distinct enemy types; party expansion increases tactical options and required team management
difficulty_curve.late_game: Boss encounters and story-specific battles increase in complexity; optional character side-quests raise difficulty ceiling for player choice
notable_spikes: Introduction of time travel mechanics (mid-game shift in scope)
Optional boss fights scaling to party level and equipment

notable_level_patterns.description: Town/settlement with shops, NPCs, story dialogue followed by field exploration and dungeon with visible enemies and puzzle-solving
Each era accessible via time travel presents entirely new overworld, dungeon layouts, enemy types, and NPCs; maintains spatial vocabulary across time periods
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Present/Past/Future transitions with their respective dungeons

notable_level_patterns.description: Visible enemies on navigation maps allow player to choose engagement; contact initiates battle on same screen rather than transition
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All field maps where enemies are pre-placed for player tactical awareness

notable_level_patterns.description: Optional story content tied to individual party members focusing on character development rather than mandatory progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-to-late game optional boss encounters and narrative branches
